_comment: Pre-retirement person with $200K employment income and expenses ($250K) that far exceed income plus savings available above the minimum floor ($5K). The brokerage is forced to sell ~$45K to cover the deficit. With $200K income, the ordinary income after standard deduction (~$169K) already exceeds the 0% LTCG threshold (~$99K), so the entire brokerage gain is taxed at 15%. Tests that: (1) forced sale CGT appears in usTaxDetail as 'Capital Gains Tax (long-term)', and (2) usTax correctly includes the 15% CGT component.
